rulings.question: Is doc-quality an advisor-routable WORKFLOW packet or pure shared backbone?
rulings.ruling: Doc-quality is an advisor-routable workflow packet inside the sk-doc parent hub, not pure shared backbone.
rulings.rationale: Current sk-doc treats Document Quality as an executable user job: enforce structure, optimize for AI assistants, validate quality, run structure/DQI extraction, detect document type, and pass validation. The sk-design audit analog confirms this shape belongs as a workflow packet resolved through hub metadata under one parent skill identity.

rulings.question: Does doc-quality warrant a /doc:quality command?
rulings.ruling: Yes. Add /doc:quality as a direct-pin command for existing documentation review, scoring, validation, and hardening; default must be report-only/recommend-only unless an explicit apply path is requested.
rulings.rationale: The /design:audit command is the exact analog: it pins the audit mode, uses it when the request is to review/score/harden an existing surface, loads the parent hub plus mode contract, emits a quality report, and never silently chains follow-up work. Doc-quality has the same job shape for markdown/documentation quality.

rulings.question: Where do optimization, hvr_rules, quick_reference, and workflows live?
rulings.ruling: Split by role: hvr_rules stays shared vocabulary/standards; quick_reference stays a shared front door but only with pointers and lightweight gates; workflows and optimization move under the doc-quality workflow as owned procedure.
rulings.rationale: Current routing separates DOC_QUALITY, OPTIMIZATION, and HVR signals, but the monolith structure is not the target architecture. The later breadth split resolves the conflict: HVR is cross-cutting writing vocabulary; optimization and workflows are execution procedure for quality/AI-context improvement; quick_reference is always-loaded navigation and should not retain deep DQI/transformation procedure.

rulings.question: Should optimization or HVR become named modes/extensions?
rulings.ruling: No. Neither becomes a workflowMode or named extension. Optimization is doc-quality-owned procedure; HVR is shared authoring vocabulary consumed by all creation and quality workflows.
rulings.rationale: The sk-design registry shows modes as explicit workflow packets with packetKind, backendKind, packet path, aliases, and advisorRouting metadata. HVR and optimization are resource scopes, not peer workflow packets; promoting them would over-decompose the hub and recreate monolith intent sprawl as modes.

artifact: Spec text: Add `doc-quality` as a first-tier workflow packet under the sk-doc parent hub. Register it with `workflowMode: doc-quality`, `packetKind: workflow`, `backendKind: reference-base`, and `advisorRouting.routingClass: metadata`; do not create a separate skill identity or packet-local graph-metadata.json. Add `/doc:quality <target> [--scope] [--score] [--apply]` as a direct-pin command that loads the sk-doc hub and the doc-quality packet, requires a concrete documentation target, and emits `Documentation Quality Report` with target, evidenceInventory, severityFindings, dqiScore, and recommendedChanges. Default command behavior is report-only/recommend-only; edits require an explicit apply path. Resource ownership: keep `hvr_rules.md` in shared; keep `quick_reference.md` in shared as the always-loaded front door with pointers only; move `workflows.md` and `optimization.md` under doc-quality as owned procedure. Preserve legacy root/global reference paths through facade symlinks for external-ref stability.
risks: Leaving optimization in shared would preserve mode procedure in the backbone and weaken the workflow-only split: .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/125-sk-doc-parent/001-research-and-canon/research/gpt5-iterations/iter-14-global-refs-split.json:1
Moving HVR into doc-quality would hide shared writing standards from creation workflows: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/SKILL.md:224; .opencode/skills/sk-doc/SKILL.md:240

Keeping deep DQI/transformation material in quick_reference would make the always-loaded shared front door procedural instead of navigational: .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/125-sk-doc-parent/001-research-and-canon/research/gpt5-iterations/iter-14-global-refs-split.json:1
